EvaluationRules.Message: FSMO role holder is unreachable
FSMO role placement issue detected

Checks.Help: Checks that all five FSMO roles (PDC Emulator, RID Master, Infrastructure Master, Schema Master, Domain Naming Master) are held by reachable DCs and follow best-practice placement guidelines.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Run: netdom query fsmo to identify current role holders. 2. Verify all role holders are reachable and healthy. 3. If a role holder is unreachable, seize the role using ntdsutil: 'roles' then 'seize <role name>'. 4. Place PDC Emulator and RID Master on the same well-resourced DC; keep Infrastructure Master on a non-GC DC in multi-domain forests.

Checks.PassMessage: All FSMO roles are available and responding

EvaluationRules.Message: FSMO role is not available - AD operations may be impacted
FSMO role holder is not responding to LDAP
FSMO availability issue detected

Checks.Help: Verifies that all FSMO role holders are online and responding. An unavailable PDC Emulator affects password changes and time sync; an unavailable RID Master prevents new object creation once the current RID pool is exhausted.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Test connectivity to the role holder: Test-NetConnection -ComputerName <RoleHolder> -Port 389. 2. If the DC is down, bring it back online or transfer the role to another DC: Move-ADDirectoryServerOperationMasterRole -Identity <DC> -OperationMasterRole <role>. 3. If the DC cannot be recovered, seize the role using ntdsutil - never bring the original holder back online after seizing. 4. Monitor role holder DCs closely to prevent recurrence.

Checks.PassMessage: All domain trusts are working correctly

EvaluationRules.Message: Domain trust relationship is broken or not responding
Trust validation issue detected

Checks.Help: Validates that all configured domain and forest trusts are functioning correctly. Broken trusts prevent users in one domain from accessing resources in the trusted domain, and can cause authentication failures for cross-domain applications.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Test the trust from both sides: netdom verify <TrustedDomain> /domain:<TrustingDomain>. 2. Reset the trust password: netdom trust <TrustedDomain> /domain:<TrustingDomain> /resetSecureChannel. 3. If trust is completely broken, delete and recreate it from Active Directory Domains and Trusts. 4. Ensure DNS name resolution works between both domains before recreating.

Checks.PassMessage: SID filtering is appropriately configured on all trusts

EvaluationRules.Message: SID filtering is not appropriately configured for trust type
SID filtering issue detected

Checks.Help: Checks that SID filtering (also called SID quarantine) is enabled on external trusts. Disabling SID filtering allows an attacker who compromises a trusted domain to use SID history to escalate privileges in the trusting domain.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Check current SID filtering status: netdom trust <TrustedDomain> /domain:<TrustingDomain> /quarantine. 2. Enable SID filtering on external trusts: netdom trust <TrustedDomain> /domain:<TrustingDomain> /quarantine:yes. 3. SID filtering is enabled by default on external trusts - only disable if required for SID history migration. 4. Forest trusts use SID filtering at the forest boundary by default - verify this is not disabled.

Checks.PassMessage: AD site topology is properly configured

EvaluationRules.Message: AD sites exist with no domain controllers assigned
AD sites exist with no subnets defined
Subnets exist that are not assigned to any AD site
Site link configuration issues detected
Site topology issue detected

Checks.Help: Reviews the AD site and subnet configuration to detect missing subnets, orphaned sites, and site link issues. Incorrect site topology causes clients to authenticate against distant DCs, increasing latency and WAN traffic.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Open Active Directory Sites and Services and review sites, subnets, and site links. 2. Add missing subnet definitions: New-ADReplicationSubnet -Name '<IP/prefix>' -Site '<SiteName>'. 3. Remove empty sites that no longer have DCs: Remove-ADReplicationSite -Identity '<SiteName>'. 4. Ensure every physical network segment has a corresponding AD subnet assigned to the correct site.

Checks.PassMessage: NTDS.dit is stored on a dedicated volume on all DCs
Checks.FailMessage: NTDS.dit is stored on the system drive on one or more DCs

EvaluationRules.Message: NTDS.dit is stored on the system drive - move to a dedicated volume
DIT file location issue detected

Checks.Help: Checks that NTDS.dit is stored on a dedicated volume separate from the Windows system files. Storing the database on the system drive risks corruption during OS updates and reduces I/O performance.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Schedule a maintenance window to move NTDS.dit. 2. Boot to DSRM: ntdsutil files move db to D:\NTDS. 3. Update the registry path: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Parameters\DSA Database file. 4. Reboot and verify AD starts correctly.

Checks.PassMessage: NTDS transaction logs are on a dedicated volume on all DCs
Checks.FailMessage: NTDS transaction log location issues detected

EvaluationRules.Message: NTDS transaction logs are on the system drive - move to a dedicated volume
NTDS transaction logs share the same drive as the database
Log file location issue detected

Checks.Help: Verifies NTDS transaction logs are on a dedicated volume. Logs on the same disk as the OS or database compete for I/O, slowing write operations and increasing corruption risk during disk failures.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Boot to DSRM: ntdsutil files move logs to E:\NTDSLogs. 2. Update registry: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Parameters\Database log files path. 3. Delete old log files from the original location after verifying AD health. 4. Reboot normally and verify replication.

Checks.PassMessage: SYSVOL is on a dedicated volume on all DCs
Checks.FailMessage: SYSVOL is stored on the system drive on one or more DCs

EvaluationRules.Message: SYSVOL is stored on the system drive - move to a dedicated volume
SYSVOL location issue detected

Checks.Help: Checks that SYSVOL is not on the system drive. SYSVOL stores GPO templates and logon scripts - keeping it on the system drive reduces performance and risks data loss if the OS volume fails.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Moving SYSVOL requires careful procedures to avoid SYSVOL inconsistency. 2. Use dfsrmig to migrate SYSVOL to DFSR if still using FRS. 3. To relocate: stop NETLOGON and DFSR services, move files, update registry SysVol path, restart services. 4. Verify SYSVOL sharing: net share SYSVOL.

Checks.PassMessage: AD garbage collection interval is within acceptable range
Checks.FailMessage: AD garbage collection interval is set too high

EvaluationRules.Message: Garbage collection interval exceeds 24 hours - deleted objects may accumulate

Checks.Help: Checks the AD garbage collection interval, which controls how often the AD database removes tombstoned (deleted) objects. Infrequent garbage collection allows deleted objects to accumulate and increase database size.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Set garbage collection interval via ADSIEdit: CN=Directory Service,CN=Windows NT,CN=Services,CN=Configuration. 2. Set garbageCollPeriod attribute to 12 (hours) - the recommended default. 3. The setting takes effect on the next garbage collection cycle. 4. Monitor database size growth to detect accumulation issues.

Checks.PassMessage: AD schema version meets the Windows Server 2016 baseline
Checks.FailMessage: AD schema version is below Windows Server 2016

EvaluationRules.Message: AD schema is below Windows Server 2016 - run adprep /forestprep to update
Schema version issue detected

Checks.Help: Reports the current AD schema version. An outdated schema prevents using newer AD features and may block OS upgrades. Schema updates are required before promoting Windows Server domain controllers of newer versions.
Checks.Remediation: 1. To update the schema run adprep /forestprep from the newest Windows Server DVD or ISO. 2. Requires Schema Admins membership. 3. Verify schema update: Get-ADObject -Identity (Get-ADRootDSE).schemaNamingContext -Properties objectVersion. 4. Schema updates are one-way and irreversible - test in a lab first.

Checks.PassMessage: Forest functional level meets the Windows Server 2016 baseline
Checks.FailMessage: Forest functional level is below Windows Server 2016

EvaluationRules.Message: Forest functional level is below Windows Server 2016 - modern features may not be available
Forest functional level issue detected

Checks.Help: Reports the forest functional level which determines which forest-wide features are available. A low forest functional level prevents enabling features like the AD Recycle Bin and Privileged Access Management.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Raise forest functional level in ADUC or via PowerShell: Set-ADForestMode -Identity <forest> -ForestMode Windows2016Forest. 2. All domains must first be at the equivalent domain functional level. 3. This change is irreversible - ensure all DCs are running compatible OS versions. 4. Test in a non-production environment first.

Checks.PassMessage: All domains meet the Windows Server 2016 functional level baseline
Checks.FailMessage: One or more domains are below Windows Server 2016 functional level

EvaluationRules.Message: Domain functional level is below Windows Server 2016 - upgrade DCs and raise the functional level
Domain functional level issue detected

Checks.Help: Reports the domain functional level which controls domain-wide features. A low functional level prevents using features like fine-grained password policies (requires 2008) and managed service accounts.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Raise domain functional level: Set-ADDomainMode -Identity <domain> -DomainMode Windows2016Domain. 2. All DCs in the domain must be running Windows Server 2016 or newer. 3. Decommission or upgrade older DCs first. 4. This change is irreversible.

Checks.PassMessage: LDAP signing is required on all domain controllers
Checks.FailMessage: LDAP signing is not required on one or more domain controllers

EvaluationRules.Message: LDAP signing is disabled on a DC - LDAP relay attacks are possible
LDAP signing is not required on one or more DCs - set LDAPServerIntegrity=2
LDAP signing configuration issue detected

Checks.Help: Checks whether DCs require LDAP signing, which prevents man-in-the-middle attacks on LDAP connections. Without required signing, attackers can intercept and modify LDAP traffic between clients and domain controllers.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Set LDAPServerIntegrity to 2 via GPO: Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Local Policies > Security Options > Domain controller: LDAP server signing requirements = Require signing. 2. Or set directly: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Parameters\LDAPServerIntegrity = 2. 3. Test applications for LDAP signing compatibility before enforcing. 4. Monitor Event ID 2886 in Directory Service log for unsigned LDAP connections.

Checks.PassMessage: LDAPS (port 636) is available on all domain controllers
Checks.FailMessage: LDAPS (port 636) is not available on one or more domain controllers

EvaluationRules.Message: LDAPS is not available on one or more DCs - install a valid SSL certificate to enable LDAPS
LDAPS configuration issue detected

Checks.Help: Tests whether LDAPS (LDAP over SSL/TLS on port 636) is available on each DC. LDAPS encrypts all LDAP traffic and is required for secure credential operations. Applications transmitting credentials should use LDAPS.
Checks.Remediation: 1. LDAPS requires a valid SSL certificate on each DC. 2. Enroll DCs in a certificate with the DC's FQDN as the Subject or SAN. 3. Use autoenrollment via an internal PKI (AD CS) for automated certificate deployment. 4. Verify LDAPS after certificate installation: Test-NetConnection -ComputerName DC01 -Port 636.

Checks.PassMessage: All RODCs are reachable and have Password Replication Policies configured

EvaluationRules.Message: One or more RODCs are unreachable - local branch authentication may be denied
One or more RODCs have no Password Replication Policy configured
RODC health issue detected

Checks.Help: Checks the health of Read-Only Domain Controllers (RODCs). RODCs are used in branch offices and DMZ environments - an unreachable RODC denies authentication to local users at that site.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Verify RODC physical connectivity and power state. 2. Check RODC replication: repadmin /showrepl <RODC>. 3. Ensure Password Replication Policy is configured: which accounts are allowed/denied password caching. 4. If RODC is permanently offline, demote via: Uninstall-ADDSDomainController -DemoteOperationMasterRole.
